text.title: API Gateway Patterns & Technology Selection Worked Example
text.summary: One end-to-end worked example showing how api-gateway-patterns produces a valid decision-record artefact.

example.title: Happy-path application
example.code:
--- artefact_id: api-gateway-patterns-acme-2026-05-23 owner: Ruslan Faion <ruslan@faion.net> adr_id: 017 pattern: bff product: kong ---
## Context B2B SaaS with three client families: web admin console, mobile partner app, and webhook receivers. Each family hits 8-12 backend services with different aggregation needs.
## Decision Adopt the BFF pattern with Kong gateways; one BFF per client family backed by a shared Kong cluster.
## Alternatives Rejected | Option | Reason rejected | |--------|-----------------| | envoy | Team has no Envoy operator; managed UX needed for solo founder. | | aws-apigw | Hetzner-hosted backend; cross-cloud egress cost too high. | | per-service | Operational fan-out unsustainable at 12 services with 1.5 ops engineers. |
## Consequences - One additional hop per request (~3-5ms added p95). - Cross-cutting concerns (auth, rate limit, observability) consolidated. - Vendor concentration on Kong — tagged partial_two_way (3 weeks rollback to Traefik).
example.note: Demonstrates the rules from 01-core-rules.xml applied to a concrete input; output validates against the schema in 02-output-contract.xml.
